entries.summary: Executed the feasible RR-002 slice in the macOS workspace by hardening Windows CI and scripted validation instead of claiming real hardware validation that was not run.
Added a headless Windows smoke mode plus package-script validation so CI can exercise the packaged executable itself without pretending tray or capture UX was validated.
Treated the Windows-targeted compile failure as a real RR-002 defect and fixed it in scope instead of deferring it behind the broader runtime-validation risk.
Kept RR-002 on phase/RR-002-windows-runtime-hardening and cherry-picked only the targeted validation-fix commit so the phase record stays isolated from unrelated feature-branch work.

Treated the inherited diagnostics-redaction failure and packaged-smoke exit-code bug as RR-002 work because they blocked the package/runtime signal, then reran DiagnosticsLoggerTests and release_smoke_test.sh on the phase branch before updating state.
Extended RR-002 package validation by checking packaged-file hashes against the publish outputs and by uploading a machine-readable Windows validation manifest plus smoke report from the CI run.

Captured the Windows takeover path in-repo with a dedicated handoff doc, a handoff script, and a CI-produced handoff artifact so Codex on Windows can continue RR-002 from repo state instead of chat-only context.
Captured the GitHub Actions Node 20 deprecation warnings from the successful phase-branch run as a separate follow-up risk instead of expanding RR-002 into workflow-runtime maintenance.

Kept RR-002 as the canonical product roadmap phase while using phase/bootstrap to add repo-local execution scaffolding, so roadmap delivery state and execution-system bootstrap work stay explicit instead of overwriting each other.
Made tools/validators/enforce-runtime-guardrails.js the single CI gatekeeper and reused the existing roadmap and state ledgers instead of creating a second validator or external-state dependency.

Because RR-002 still needs real Windows hardware validation that cannot be completed honestly from this macOS workspace, advanced the next unblocked risk-remediation slice by upgrading core workflow actions to Node24-compatible majors and teaching the runtime guardrails validator to enforce those versions across both CI and release workflows.

Treated the stale dmgbuild shebang as a release-environment defect, switched scripts/build_dmg.sh to run dmgbuild through the virtualenv Python module, and revalidated release_smoke_test.sh plus unsigned DMG packaging before recording release readiness.

Hardened the local packaging entrypoint by making scripts/build_dmg.sh run scripts/release_smoke_test.sh by default, which carries the startup keychain smoke into DMG preflight; then reran workflow lint and unsigned DMG packaging through that exact path before recording release status.

Closed OPS-011 after hosted CI run 23988211616 and hosted Release workflow run 23988213079 both succeeded on commit eca6ba9; upgraded actions/setup-python to v6 when the first hosted Release run still emitted a Node 20 deprecation annotation.

Because RR-002 is still blocked on real Windows hardware validation, started OPS-012 by patching the docs-site dependency graph locally with targeted overrides and direct tarball replacements, validated the patched versions with npm ls and a production Docusaurus build, and logged the local npm 11 / Node 25 EBADF fetch failure as a separate environment risk instead of treating it as successful package-manager remediation.

Validated hosted CI run 23988782016 on commit 5369212 after pushing the docs-site dependency remediation, then narrowed the remaining OPS-012 work to default-branch promotion for alert closure and the unresolved local npm 11 / Node 25 EBADF environment defect.

Replaced the broken local npm 11 / Node 25 docs-site install path with a repo-local Node 22 wrapper pinned in site/.node-version, then validated a clean docs-site reinstall and production build through that path before closing the environment risk.

Promoted the original OPS-012 remediation to main via PR #6, verified the original four default-branch Dependabot alerts were gone, and closed RISK-SEC-001 instead of leaving the already-cleared alert set open.

Split the two newly surfaced default-branch lodash alerts into a new follow-up risk and task inside OPS-012, and pinned lodash to the non-deprecated patched release 4.18.1 after 4.18.0 reported a registry deprecation notice during the first branch-local test.
